title: Groundwork Of Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: Groundwork Of Evangelion             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Fri Apr 29, 2005 3:33 pm I am thinking to buy these artbooks,despite their high price(40$,hoo boy that's a lot!) Does anyone of you own them,so he can tell me if they really worth it?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Apr 29, 2005 3:46 pm The ground work, like the storyboard books, or filmbooks, are a vague bunch. I think the groundworks are interesting to look at, and a good reference for hardcore fans. I think Reichu is one of the few people I know to have put them to good use. I think she could give you a better opinion than I. Personally, I don't think they're of interest to anyone other than the hardcore Eva collector (like myself). There's quite a few books I would recommend over the Groundworks.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Apr 29, 2005 4:46 pm They aren't going to be very interesting unless you're interested in animation or the anime production process. For me, they have definitely been worthwhile -- but, I suspect, wouldn't appeal to most for any more than one flip-through. For the hardcore fan, indeed.

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Fri Apr 29, 2005 5:28 pm So they are all sketches with pencil?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Apr 29, 2005 5:38 pm A few color plates in the beginning of each one, but it's hardly worth forking over the incredible cover price just for those.

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Fri Apr 29, 2005 5:51 pm Reichu wrote: A few color plates in the beginning of each one, but it's hardly worth forking over the incredible cover price just for those.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Apr 29, 2005 7:18 pm Yeah, but the first volume has barely any Asuka in it. ;)

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Sat Apr 30, 2005 4:55 am Reichu wrote: Yeah, but the first volume has barely any Asuka in it. ;)

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Apr 30, 2005 8:40 am I guess any besides Groundwork 1... I don't have them with me right now, so I can't issue a proper recommendation.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Sat Apr 30, 2005 8:58 am the_seventh_child wrote: Reichu wrote: Yeah, but the first volume has barely any Asuka in it. ;)

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Sat Apr 30, 2005 10:11 am DatDude wrote: Their are times we thing the same thoughs seventh :)

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Apr 30, 2005 10:14 am Will you decide on a dad-gum avatar already!?!? :shock:

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Sat Apr 30, 2005 10:19 am Reichu wrote: Will you decide on a dad-gum avatar already!?!? :shock:

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Apr 30, 2005 10:21 am Well, one naked Eva head is just as good as another!

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Sat Apr 30, 2005 10:23 am Reichu wrote: Well, one naked Eva head is just as good as another!

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Apr 30, 2005 10:52 am Why? Was Azrael a better looker than "Barb"?

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Sat Apr 30, 2005 12:27 pm Reichu wrote: Why? Was Azrael a better looker than "Barb"?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Apr 30, 2005 4:21 pm #1 - 01-08. #2 - 09-19. #3 - 20-26. If you want Asuka, you want #2 or #3.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Apr 30, 2005 4:32 pm I am shocked by your forgetfulness, Monkey. Groundwork of Movie #1 - DEATH, #25' Groundwork of Movie #2 - #26', EoE production drawings[/quote]

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Sat Apr 30, 2005 5:35 pm Alright guys,i get it!Thanks
